He urged Vijayan to avoid making statements without full knowledge of the situation.]]></description><link><![CDATA[https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/bengaluru/we-are-not-into-bulldozers-dk-shivakumar-hits-back-at-kerala-cm-over-bengaluru-demolition-drive/articleshow/126201940.cms]]></link><guid><![CDATA[https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/bengaluru/we-are-not-into-bulldozers-dk-shivakumar-hits-back-at-kerala-cm-over-bengaluru-demolition-drive/articleshow/126201940.cms]]></guid><pubDate>Sat, 27 Dec 2025 13:45:53 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI CITY DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="53092" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-126201985,imgsize-53092.cms"/></item><item><title><![CDATA[Astronomers spot wobbling jets on rare interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS]]></title><description><![CDATA[Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS is revealing surprising details about its journey. Scientists observed unusual, wobbling jet-like features in its sun-facing anti-tail, suggesting a rotation period of about 15.5 hours. This offers a rare glimpse into the behaviour of a pristine object from another star system reacting to solar heat for the first time, aiding our understanding of planetary formation elsewhere.]]></description><link><![CDATA[https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/science/astronomers-spot-wobbling-jets-on-rare-interstellar-comet-3i/atlas/articleshow/126202904.cms]]></link><guid><![CDATA[https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/science/astronomers-spot-wobbling-jets-on-rare-interstellar-comet-3i/atlas/articleshow/126202904.cms]]></guid><pubDate>Sat, 27 Dec 2025 15:33:00 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI SCIENCE DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="22122" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-126202996,imgsize-22122.cms"/></item><item><title><![CDATA[Israel approves, US does not: Why Somaliland recognition has triggered backlash]]></title><description><![CDATA[Israel's recognition of Somaliland as an independent state has sparked significant diplomatic backlash across Africa and the Middle East. This move reopens the debate over the de facto state, which declared independence from Somalia in 1991 and has its own government and institutions but lacks international recognition.]]></description><link><![CDATA[https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/world/rest-of-world/israel-approves-us-does-not-why-somaliland-recognition-has-triggered-backlash-and-why-it-matters/articleshow/126199747.cms]]></link><guid><![CDATA[https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/world/rest-of-world/israel-approves-us-does-not-why-somaliland-recognition-has-triggered-backlash-and-why-it-matters/articleshow/126199747.cms]]></guid><pubDate>Sat, 27 Dec 2025 10:34:32 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI WORLD DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="773381" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-126199957,imgsize-773381.cms"/></item><item><title><![CDATA[FaZe Clan faces major shake-up as five top streamers announce exits]]></title><description><![CDATA[Several well-known Twitch streamers have officially left FaZe Clan. The announcements were made on December 25, 2025. Along with Stable Ronaldo, four other members confirmed they were no longer part of FaZe Clan. Rani “Stable Ronaldo” was the first to confirm his exit through a post on X. Soon after, other members followed with similar messages. Their announcements were brief but emotional in tone.]]></description><link><![CDATA[https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/world/us-streamers/faze-clan-faces-major-shake-up-list-of-all-five-top-streamers-who-have-announced-exit-from-the-organization/articleshow/126189373.cms]]></link><guid><![CDATA[https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/world/us-streamers/faze-clan-faces-major-shake-up-list-of-all-five-top-streamers-who-have-announced-exit-from-the-organization/articleshow/126189373.cms]]></guid><pubDate>Fri, 26 Dec 2025 18:00:00 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>GLOBAL SPORTS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="898660" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-126189371,imgsize-898660.cms"/></item></channel></rss>

      <description><![CDATA[As India enters 2026, the alcoholic beverages industry is moving into a more mature phase of growth. CRISIL Ratings projects 8–10% revenue growth in FY26, taking the industry to ₹5,30,000 crore. However, the sector continues to operate within a fragmented, state-level regulatory framework. A more balanced and predictable policy environment would support innovation, employment, and sustained revenue growth, notes Diageo India's Chief Commercial Officer Jagbir Singh Sidhu.]]></description>

      <title><![CDATA[Rare earths and the new power play: How supply chains reshaped global geopolitics in 2025]]></title>
      <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/world/rare-earth-critical-minerals-geopolitics-year-ender-2025-19802539.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[From EV factories to defence supply chains, rare earth shortages exposed how deeply the global economy depends on a handful of strategic minerals. China’s export controls in 2025 triggered price shocks, production cuts, and a worldwide race to build alternative supply chains, pushing governments and corporations to rethink resource security.]]></description>

      <description><![CDATA[The FMCG sector saw a sharp rise in leadership changes in 2025 across Indian and global companies as boards pushed for quicker results.     <item>
      <title><![CDATA[Amit Shah urges realtors to adopt green norms, build low-cost homes]]></title>
      <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/business/amit-shah-green-building-norms-housing-sector-credai-19797432.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[Addressing real estate developers at a CREDAI conclave, Home Minister Amit Shah underscored the need for transparency in land markets, cautioned against speculative land banking, and said recent GST cuts on building materials could lower project costs by 5–7%, enabling builders to offer better amenities without compromising sustainability goals.]]></description>

      <description><![CDATA[Pallav Banerjee, MD of Pearl Global, detailed the impact of the 25% US penalty tariff on its Indian operations, which has led to pressure for discounts and a shift in manufacturing. The company is mitigating this by diversifying its geographical manufacturing base and reducing US market exposure to below 50%. Banerjee expressed strong optimism for upcoming Free Trade Agreements with the UK and EU, which he believes will make India competitive and drive significant growth.]]></description>
